heading: Run a Query on the Materialized View
body:
In this section, you can learn how to run a query against the newly updated and indexed
`monthlyPhoneTransactions` collection.

Open MongoDB Compass and connect to your cluster. For detailed
instructions on connecting, see [Compass](atlas-connect-via-compass).

On the Database screen, click the `sample_supplies`
database and then click the `monthlyPhoneTransactions` collection.

The following query counts the number of months in monthlyPhoneTransactions
with total sales greater than or equal to `10000` dollars.

To run this query in MongoDB Compass:

- Click the Aggregations tab.

- Click Select..., then configure each of the
  following pipeline stages by selecting the stage from the
  dropdown and adding the query for that stage. Click
  Add Stage to add additional stages.

  | Pipeline Stage | Query                                                                                   |
  | -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
  | $search        | \{ "index": "monthlySalesIndex", "range": \{ "gt": 10000, "path": \["sales\_price"] } } |
  | $count         | "months\_w\_over\_10000"                                                                |

  If you [enabled](compass/aggregation-pipeline-builder)
  Auto Preview, MongoDB Compass displays the following
  result:

  ```json
  {
    "months_w_over_10000": 4
  }
  ```

The above query returns `4`, indicating that only 4 months out of all the months in the
`monthlyPhoneTransactions` materialized view had total sales
greater than or equal to 10000 dollars. This result reflects data
from both the `sample_supplies.sales` and
`sample_supplies.purchaseOrders` collections.

This page contains tutorials that demonstrate the
different ways you can run MongoDB Search queries across multiple collections:

- To join collections and search across them, use [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) with [$search](pipe.$search).

- To combine search results from multiple collections, use [$unionWith](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/unionWith) with [$search](pipe.$search).

- To consolidate multiple collections so you can index and search them together, use
  materialized views.

Before you begin, ensure that your cluster meets the
requirements described in the [Prerequisites](fts-design-patterns-prereqs).

Starting in v6.0, the MongoDB [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) aggregation stage
supports [$search](pipe.$search) inside the [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup)
`pipeline` option. Using [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup), you can join
multiple collections in the same database at query-time and run a
[$search](pipe.$search) query to further narrow down your search.

This tutorial demonstrates how to run a [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) query
with [$search](pipe.$search) against the `accounts` and `customers`
collections in the `sample_analytics` database.
It takes you through the following steps:

- Create a default MongoDB Search index on the `accounts`
  collection in the `sample_analytics` database.

- Run a [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) query with [$search](pipe.$search) to find
  customers from the `customers` collection whose accounts have
  purchased both `CurrencyService` and `InvestmentStock` products
  in the `accounts` collection.
